description: Model-selection redesign: provider-first models+routing config block (hard break with 5 legacy surfaces), capability tiers (frontier/standard/cost-efficient/cheapest) with anchor-window selection, per-provider effort tables incl. codex -c model_reasoning_effort, role split + difficulty spillover + quota-aware failover, tier-based escalation ladders, provider stamping (migration v20). Review cascade DEFERRED to tasks/prd-review-cascade.md; engine-side frontier forcing REMOVED (FR-011 generator guidance already delivered — do NOT implement it here).

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests pass per iteration; full `cargo test` green at milestones
`cargo run --bin gen-docs -- --check` passes after any src/loop_engine/model.rs constant/table change
All tasks.status writes via TaskLifecycle verbs — no raw UPDATEs
Model-ID literals live only in src/loop_engine/model.rs
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required by the PRD hard break
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); tier abstraction makes every future model launch a config edit
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every PRD Known Edge Case has a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones — milestones leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Resolution determinism — resolve_execution_plan is a pure function of (task fields, config); identical inputs → identical plan in sequential and wave paths
Channel discipline — runner_overrides (permanent promotion) and provider_blackouts (temporary quota reroute) are strictly separate; blackout never touches overrides
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: resolve_execution_plan (or any resolution-path function) writing tasks.model — escalation/promotion paths are the ONLY writers (escape-valve contract)
BlackoutState persisted to disk/DB, or read/written by promote_once / runner_overrides paths
Substring-based model→tier or model→provider matching anywhere — provider inference stays token-equality, tier lookup is config exact-match
A second difficulty/effort normalizer — reuse the one normalize_difficulty everywhere
Legacy alias tier keys (opus/sonnet/haiku) accepted in the new tiers map
Codex inferred from a model string — Codex routing is config-explicit only (byIdPrefix route or taskClasses)
Raw UPDATE on tasks.status — all status writes via TaskLifecycle verbs
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that hand-build config maps not matching the FR-001 JSON schema — use production-shaped fixtures
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong (CONFIG ERRORs must name the offending key and the accepted set)
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: CapabilityTier ordered enum with strict CapabilityTier::parse (typos → Err naming the accepted set) and as_str() emitting kebab-case exactly ('cost-efficient', not 'cost_efficient')

ModelsConfig/ProviderConfig/RoutingConfig deserialize the FR-001 canonical JSON verbatim; field defaults per FR-001 (claude enabled, grok/codex disabled, anchor=standard, primaryProvider=claude); field-wise merge so {"grok":{"enabled":true}} is a complete opt-in

ResolvedModelsConfig::model_for(Provider, CapabilityTier) -> Option<&str> with sparse-ladder clamping: nearest defined tier, down first then up; tiers null value = route-with-no-model-flag, absent = undefined rung
ResolvedModelsConfig::tier_of(Provider, &str) -> Option<CapabilityTier> strips the [1m] suffix before exact match (claude-fable-5[1m] → Frontier)
ResolvedModelsConfig::effort_for(Provider, Option<&str>) uses the existing normalize_difficulty — no second normalizer
Exhaustive clamping matrix tests: single-tier ladder, gap ladder (cheapest+frontier only), anchor=cheapest+high / anchor=frontier+low clamped at ladder ends (offset never wraps)

validate_models_config is pure (no I/O) and rejects: unknown/legacy-alias tier keys, disabled primaryProvider, ambiguous reverse model lookup, codex effort xhigh, fallback to self or disabled provider, routes referencing disabled providers, premature routing.reviewCascade key ('not yet supported' note)
detect_legacy_model_keys(&serde_json::Value) -> Vec<&'static str> names each legacy key found (defaultModel, reviewModel, primaryRunner, fallbackRunner)
probe_enabled_provider_binaries(&ResolvedModelsConfig) is a separate function, enabled-gated, NEVER invoked from validate_models_config
FABLE_MODEL and ONE_M_SUFFIX constants added to src/loop_engine/model.rs; tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs regex gains fable
CONTRACT: signatures match the PRD Public Contracts table exactly — every downstream task compiles against them unchanged
userStories.completionCheck: cargo test capability_tier && cargo test no_hardcoded_models
userStories.description: The keystone abstraction every downstream task consumes. Define CapabilityTier {Cheapest, CostEfficient, Standard, Frontier} (ordered), ModelsConfig/ProviderConfig/RoutingConfig serde matching the FR-001 canonical schema, built-in defaults with field-wise merge, ResolvedModelsConfig (model_for / tier_of / effort_for with sparse-ladder clamping), ExecutionPlan {provider, model, tier, effort}, FABLE_MODEL + ONE_M_SUFFIX constants, validate_models_config (pure — schema/semantic validation ONLY, no I/O), detect_legacy_model_keys, and probe_enabled_provider_binaries as a SEPARATE enabled-gated function never called from the validator. Signatures must match the PRD §6 Public Contracts table exactly.
userStories.notes: KEYSTONE TASK (FR-011 generator rule): highest transitive fan-out — every FEAT/REFACTOR in this PRD consumes these signatures; stamped estimatedEffort=high so the loop escalates the model. Strictly pure: no filesystem, no PATH probes inside the validator. Learning 4959: prefix/ID matching uses dash-boundary token matching, never substring. Learning 4729: no_hardcoded_models keeps literals in model.rs only. Data contract: cfg.providers is HashMap<String, ProviderConfig> (lowercase provider-name keys); p.tiers is HashMap<String, Option<String>> (kebab-case tier keys → model id). Modularity rule (PRD §2.6): anchor/tier derivation lives in exactly ONE function shared by spawn site and every recovery path.
qualityDimensions: Strict parse for all config enums — typos are CONFIG ERRORs naming the accepted set, never silent fall-throughs
Pure functions: validate_models_config performs zero I/O; binary probes live in a separate enabled-gated function
Sparse-ladder clamping is deterministic and pinned by an exhaustive test matrix (down first, then up)
tier_of is config exact-match after [1m] strip — substring matching is dead
Model-ID literals only in model.rs; FABLE_MODEL covered by the no_hardcoded_models regression regex
Allocation-light: resolution structures built once per run, cheap lookups after
userStories.title: Foundation: CapabilityTier, models/routing config types, ResolvedModelsConfig, ExecutionPlan (pure, no I/O)

acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: default config + anchor=standard resolves low→claude-sonnet-4-6, medium→claude-opus-4-8, high→claude-fable-5; anchor=cost-efficient shifts to haiku/sonnet/opus
Edge case tests cover all five entries in edgeCases (1:1)
Known-bad discriminator: a substring-style tier lookup (e.g. model id 'my-claude-fable-5-custom') must NOT reverse-map to any tier — only exact config match (post-[1m]-strip) does
Invariant assertions: clamping never wraps past ladder ends; tier_of(model_for(p, t)) round-trips for every defined rung; provider_for_model token-equality unchanged (groq-llama-* → Claude)
Structural assertion: serde_json::from_value::<ModelsConfig>(FR-001 canonical JSON).is_ok() — fixture derives field names from the actual struct definitions, never invented
Test file compiles and runs green against CONTRACT-001
userStories.description: TDD edge-case suite against the CONTRACT-001 pure surface. Covers the PRD Known Edge Cases that live entirely in the config/tier layer. Tests compile against CONTRACT-001 signatures and pass immediately (the foundation implements them) — they exist to pin the contract so later FEATs cannot regress it.
edgeCases: claude-fable-5[1m] in tasks.model: tier_of strips [1m] before exact match → reverse-maps to Frontier
Sparse grok ladder (only standard defined), anchor window lands on frontier: clamp prefers stepping down, then up
Anchor=cheapest + difficulty=high and anchor=frontier + difficulty=low: clamped to cheapest/frontier respectively — offset never wraps
Single-tier ladder (codex default) and gap ladder (only cheapest + frontier defined): single tier always wins; gap ladders clamp down-first across the gap, then up — exhaustive matrix
groq-llama-* model string: token-equality keeps it Claude — substring matching would mis-route to xAI Grok runner

failureModes.cause: Ambiguous reverse model lookup (same id in two providers' ladders)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: validate_models_config returns CONFIG ERROR naming the model id and both providers

failureModes.cause: Lookup on a provider with an empty/undefined ladder

invariants: Clamping never wraps past ladder ends
tier_of(model_for(p, t)) == Some(t) for every defined rung (round-trip)
provider_for_model token-equality behavior is byte-identical before/after this PRD
userStories.notes: TDD: pin the contract before downstream FEATs build on it. Use production-shaped fixtures (the FR-001 JSON block verbatim from the PRD), NEVER hand-built maps — wrong key format (snake_case vs kebab-case tier keys) silently passes otherwise. Data contract: JSON tier keys are kebab-case strings ('cost-efficient'); CapabilityTier::as_str() must emit exactly that.
qualityDimensions: Tests are specific enough to reject wrong implementations (known-bad discriminator present)
Fixtures are production-shaped: FR-001 canonical JSON, real struct field names
Every assertion verifies content (exact model id / tier), not just is_some()
userStories.title: Pure-layer edge-case tests: tier matrix, [1m] strip, ladder clamps, token-equality

acceptanceCriteria: Edge case tests cover all four entries in edgeCases (1:1), each mapped to its owning FEAT in the #[ignore] note

Escape-valve test skeleton: anchor-resolved task (NULL tasks.model) → overflow → escalation writes tasks.model → operator edit → six-channel clear fires exactly once with one stderr line (un-ignored by FEAT-004)

Deferral-first test skeleton: wave whose only candidates are quota-deferred waits for reset and NEVER increments the stale tracker — parity-tested in BOTH sequential and wave paths (un-ignored by FEAT-008)
Codex effort test skeleton: difficulty=high on a codex route caps effort at high; -c model_reasoning_effort=<level> appears BEFORE the exec subcommand in argv (un-ignored by FEAT-006)
Legacy-key coverage skeleton: loop/batch entry hard-errors naming each key; read_project_config warns once on stderr and proceeds; models mutating verbs hard-error (un-ignored by FEAT-002)
Known-bad discriminator: a blackout-reroute test asserting runner_overrides remains UNTOUCHED — an implementation that promotes via overrides fails this test
All non-ignored assertions compile and run green; ignored tests compile
userStories.description: TDD suite for the engine-level PRD Known Edge Cases. Write tests against EXISTING engine functions (invalidate_stale_overrides, build_codex_argv, handle_no_eligible_tasks, config read paths) plus CONTRACT-001 types. Tests asserting not-yet-implemented behavior are #[ignore = "un-ignored by FEAT-00X"] with the owning task named; FEAT-002/004/006/008 un-ignore and make them pass.
edgeCases: Anchor-resolved task (NULL tasks.model) → overflow → escalation → operator edit: escape-valve snapshot recorded NULL; comparison must still fire the six-channel clear with one stderr line
Wave where ALL candidates are quota-deferred: deferral-first branch waits for reset; stale counter untouched (empty selection historically fed the stale-abort counter)
Codex route + difficulty=high: xhigh must never reach codex — validation caps codex effort at high; -c model_reasoning_effort= precedes exec
Legacy config keys + non-loop command (recall, models show): warn-and-proceed / detect-and-instruct; hard error ONLY at loop/batch entry + models mutating verbs

failureModes.cause: Claude RateLimit with no spillover-eligible work

Learning 3927: handle_no_eligible_tasks (wave path) historically went straight to the stale tracker on empty selection — the documented false-abort bug class this PRD's deferral-first branch fixes. Learning 4921/4672: promote_once owns the single runner_overrides idempotency guard; blackout channel must never read/write it. Use production-shaped fixtures (render via tests/common/mod.rs::render_fixture_tmpl where model placeholders are needed).

Delete legacy ProjectConfig fields (defaultModel, reviewModel, primaryRunner, fallbackRunner) and ALL migration machinery (migrate_project_config_value, update_project_config_format, canonical_baseline_tier_key, serde aliases). Add default_model warning sites: loop init / batch init / loop run warn when PRD JSON or prd_metadata carries default_model; prd_metadata.default_model column STAYS, exports/imports round-trip verbatim, resolution chain stops reading it.

The interim mutating-verb guard keeps old verbs safe until FEAT-009 deletes them. Grep for every read site of the four legacy fields before deleting (learning 4915: systematic construction/usage-site discovery — production, test helpers, fixtures).

Store lowercase provider names via Provider::as_str ('claude'/'grok'/'codex'), never model strings, in completed_by_provider.

Stamp in the completion arm of process_iteration_output — single home, learning #4865/#4186 (single-home coordinators).

acceptanceCriteria: Single resolve_execution_plan called by prompt/sequential.rs and prompt/slot.rs — no second resolution path; reaction-parity pinned (identical inputs → identical plan both paths)
Precedence table test: every rung beats all lower rungs (explicit model > byIdPrefix > class route > blackout reroute > anchor window)
resolve_execution_plan NEVER writes tasks.model; escalation/promotion paths in recovery.rs continue writing it unchanged (escape-valve contract)

invalidate_stale_overrides handles NULL-original semantics: anchor-resolved task escalated then operator-edited → six-channel clear fires, one stderr line (TEST-INIT-002 escape-valve test un-ignored and green)
classify_task is the SSoT: rung 3 and spillover eligibility both consume it; unmatched IDs → class default; review class built-in and not redefinable
Consistency test: SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES ⊆ classify.implementation defaults — flags drift between the prefix sets
byDifficulty matched lowercase-trimmed via the same normalize_difficulty used by effort lookup — no second normalizer
ctx.effort_overrides (overflow downgrade) still wins over plan effort at spawn
apply_review_model_override deleted from iteration.rs and wave_scheduler.rs call sites; review routing flows from rung 3

Explicit tasks.model and routing.byIdPrefix win over class routes (US-004 AC); high-difficulty implementation stays on claude under default routing

semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Config exact-match after [1m] strip — substring dies
Cannot express Codex (invariant); codex pinning is route-only via models route

userStories.description: The 6-rung precedence chain (FR-003) as a single pure function resolve_execution_plan(ctx: &PlanContext) -> ExecutionPlan, called by BOTH prompt builders (sequential.rs + slot.rs). Rungs: (1) explicit tasks.model → provider via provider_for_model, tier via tier_of; (2) routing.byIdPrefix; (3) task class → provider preference + forced tier (review/planning → frontier) with byDifficulty remap; (4) quota-blackout reroute (spillover-eligible only, derived per pass, never stored); (5) anchor window (difficulty offset low→anchor−1 / medium→anchor / high→anchor+1, clamped); (6) tier → model via model_for; effort last from the FINAL provider's table. Name rungs in code by constant, not ordinal (the deferred cascade PRD inserts a rung between 1 and 2). classify_task(id, &RoutingConfig) -> TaskClass built on id_body_matches_prefix is the classification SSoT consumed by rung 3 AND spillover eligibility. is_review_class → is_frontier_class (review class built-in: CODE-REVIEW-, MILESTONE-FINAL, REVIEW-, excluding REFACTOR-REVIEW; not redefinable via classify). resolve_iteration_model rewritten on tier_of (None < Some(Cheapest); last-wins tie-break preserved). Thread ExecutionPlan through SlotPromptBundle, resolve_effective_runner input, wave_scheduler, iteration.rs — delete apply_review_model_override call sites (plan already carries review routing).
userStories.notes: Heart of the PRD. Learning 3019/3736: check_override_invalidation keys off tasks.model vs ctx.overflow_original_task_model — NULL-original is the new case to handle explicitly. Learning 4913: sequential/wave parity requires matching parameter threading — verify every input influencing the plan reaches both builders. Learning 4915: adding fields to SlotPromptBundle requires systematic construction-site discovery (production + test helpers + fixtures). Old fns (resolve_task_execution_target, resolve_task_model, compute_baseline_model) remain in place until REFACTOR-005 deletes them — integration tests still reference them, so no dead-code warnings; do NOT delete here. Data contract: ExecutionPlan.effort: Option<String>; spawn-time precedence: ctx.effort_overrides.get(task_id) wins over plan.effort.
qualityDimensions: Resolution determinism: pure function of (task fields, config); zero hidden state
Rungs named by constant, not ordinal — cascade PRD inserts between 1 and 2 without renumbering churn
Allocation-light on the per-slot hot path: config resolved once per run, cached
Escape-valve contract: resolution never writes tasks.model; NULL-original handled explicitly
One classifier, one normalizer — no parallel prefix sets or difficulty parsing
userStories.title: Resolution rewrite: resolve_execution_plan + classify_task + anchor window + is_frontier_class, wired into both prompt builders

acceptanceCriteria: Both prompt builders verified to call resolve_execution_plan on the production path (not bypassed by test mocks)
No resolution-path function writes tasks.model (grep-verified)
validate_models_config performs zero I/O (grep for fs/PATH usage)
Stamping single-home verified: process_iteration_output is the only writer of completed_by_provider
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx / WIRE-FIX-xxx tasks added via task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-1
userStories.description: Review CONTRACT-001, FEAT-002, FEAT-003, FEAT-004 with full context: escape-valve contract adherence (no resolution-path writes to tasks.model), channel discipline (no blackout/override cross-talk yet), purity of validate_models_config, strict-parse coverage, error-message actionability, wiring reachability (resolve_execution_plan actually called from BOTH prompt builders in production paths, not just tests), qualityDimensions satisfied, no unwrap() in production code.
userStories.notes: For each issue: echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-1 — atomic DB+JSON sync, no manual edit. If no issues, emit <task-status>CODE-REVIEW-1:done</task-status> with a one-line progress note. Use the rust-python-code-reviewer agent.
qualityDimensions: Review verifies contracts (escape valve, purity, single-home stamping) by grep/read, not by trusting task notes

acceptanceCriteria: Implementation matches the CONFIRMED spike values (key model_reasoning_effort, before-exec placement) — no re-verification needed; cap-at-high validation error message states it is a policy cap (CLI accepts xhigh)
build_codex_argv emits -c model_reasoning_effort=<level> BEFORE exec; existing argv structure otherwise byte-identical
CodexRunner::supports(Effort) == true; capability matrix updated

userStories.description: Flip CodexRunner::supports(Effort) to true; build_codex_argv gains -c model_reasoning_effort=<level> positioned BEFORE the exec subcommand. SPIKE ALREADY RESOLVED pre-loop (2026-06-09, vs pinned codex-cli 0.136.0): key = model_reasoning_effort; CLI-accepted levels = none|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh; -c valid both before and after exec — before-exec placement pinned. The CLI ACCEPTS xhigh: the validation cap at high is deliberate cost/latency POLICY (operator decision 2026-06-09), not a CLI constraint — do NOT re-run the spike, and do NOT loosen the cap. Per-provider effort: claude/grok from their own tables via --effort; codex effort values validated ∈ {low, medium, high} (xhigh rejected by CONTRACT-001 validation, error message says 'by policy').
userStories.notes: PRD Open Question RESOLVED 2026-06-09 (pre-loop spike, codex-cli 0.136.0): verification method was --strict-config with an invalid value (errors pre-API enumerating the accepted set) + unknown-key tripwire (proves valid values parse). Do not re-run. Learning 4552/4983: protected-state guard sites (iteration.rs, slot.rs, wave_scheduler.rs) must remain wrapped.
qualityDimensions: Spike-verified (resolved 2026-06-09): key name, levels, position confirmed against pinned codex-cli 0.136.0; cap-at-high is policy, surfaced in the validation message

Effort decoupled from tier: per-provider tables, no global EFFORT_FOR_DIFFICULTY consumption on codex path

semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Unchanged; keeps higher precedence than blackout reroute

userStories.description: FR-008: BlackoutState newtype living ONLY on IterationContext (in-memory, self-expiring, provider-keyed map though Claude-only in v1) with the three documented rules: never persisted, never read/written by promote_once/runner_overrides paths, set only by account-level rate-limit signals. On Claude RateLimit with spillover-eligible todo work: record blackout from parse_reset_from_output, skip the wait, return RerouteAndRetry (budget give-back; NO merge-fail-counter zeroing). Add ProceedWithSpillover variant. select_parallel_group gains excluded_ids: &HashSet<String> (+ sequential equivalent). Deferral-first branch in handle_no_eligible_tasks AND the sequential no-eligible branch: when all candidates are quota-deferred, wait for reset (reuse wait_for_usage_reset), clear blackout, return WITHOUT stale increment / auto-recovery churn / drained classification. Spillover eligibility = classify_task == implementation AND difficulty ≤ spillover.maxDifficulty AND no runner override.
userStories.notes: Learning 3927 is the bug class this prevents: wave-mode empty-group path went straight to the stale tracker, causing false stale-abort. Learning 4171: wave-mode rate-limit reaction fires once per wave (post-wave aggregation), not per slot — hook the blackout recording there. Learning 4921/4672: promote_once is the only permanent-promotion guard; the blackout channel is derived per pass, never stored in overrides. Reactions single-home contract applies (account.rs coordinators, learning 4865/4186).
qualityDimensions: Channel discipline type-enforced: BlackoutState newtype, three rules in the doc comment, consistency pinned by tests
Deferral-first ordering is structural (branch position), not flag-guarded
Selection stays provider-agnostic — only hard exclusion added, no preference weighting (non-goal)
Parity: sequential and wave no-eligible branches behave identically (learning 4913)
userStories.title: Quota-aware failover: provider_blackouts channel, RerouteAndRetry/ProceedWithSpillover, excluded_ids, deferral-first branch
